name: Priority Bank
description_short: Priority Bank is an FDIC-insured community bank offering checking, savings, loans, and insurance services across Arkansas locations with a focus on Kasasa rewards accounts.
description_long:
Priority Bank operates as a traditional community bank serving customers in Arkansas, primarily in the Fayetteville, Ozark, and Prairie Grove areas. The bank emphasizes a people-first philosophy captured in their tagline "We Value People More Than Profits" and maintains FDIC insurance backing all deposits with the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business financial products. On the consumer side, they provide multiple checking account options (including free Kasasa Cash Checking and Kasasa Cash Back Checking), personal savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, and youth savings products. Their lending portfolio includes personal loans, auto loans, home mortgages, and credit cards. For business customers, Priority Bank offers business checking and savings accounts, business money market accounts, business CDs, commercial real estate loans, small business loans, business lines of credit, and agriculture loans. Additional services include online and mobile banking, bill pay, mobile payments, identity theft protection through Kasasa Protect, insurance services, merchant card services, and cash management tools.

Priority Bank differentiates itself through its Kasasa-branded rewards checking accounts, which allow customers to earn cash rewards and receive refunds on ATM withdrawal fees nationwide. The bank also emphasizes accessibility (AudioEye-enabled website), provides insurance shopping tools promising savings of up to $500 on homeowner's and auto insurance, and markets its mortgage expertise to local customers. Their mobile banking platform and online account opening process represent modern digital conveniences.

As a regional community bank, Priority Bank serves a specific geographic footprint rather than operating nationally. While comprehensive in product offerings, customers should verify specific rates, terms, and product availability at their local branch. The bank's emphasis on local presence and personalized service appeals to community-focused banking, though this regional limitation may not suit customers requiring national branch access or those seeking the highest promotional rates available in the broader market. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts (Kasasa Cash, Kasasa Cash Back, NOW, Priority, Free, Platinum)
Personal savings accounts including youth savings and money market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for both personal and business customers
Commercial real estate loans and small business loans
Business lines of credit and agriculture loans
Online banking and bill pay services
Insurance services (homeowner's and auto insurance shopping)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Free Kasasa Cash Checking and Kasasa Cash Back Checking accounts with cash rewards and nationwide ATM fee refunds
Wide range of products including personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, business loans, and agriculture loans
Kasasa Protect identity theft protection service included with qualifying accounts
Mobile banking and mobile payments available through smartphone/tablet app
Online account opening process for quick account setup
Insurance shopping services partnered to help save on homeowner's and auto insurance
Local mortgage expertise with in-house loan officers serving Hog Country region
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Arkansas (Fayetteville, Ozark, Prairie Grove areas) with no national branch network
Website does not clearly display current interest rates, APYs, or loan terms for easy comparison
No mention of online-exclusive or promotional rates that compete with national digital banks
Limited information about credit card offerings and rewards structure
Fraud alert prominently featured suggests potential past security concerns requiring customer awareness
best_for: Arkansas residents seeking community banking with local branch access and personalized service
Customers wanting Kasasa rewards checking with cash-back benefits and nationwide ATM fee refunds
Small business owners needing local business lending and cash management services
Homebuyers in the Fayetteville/Ozark region seeking mortgage loans from local loan officers
diagnosis: Priority Bank is best for Arkansas residents seeking traditional community banking with FDIC protection, Kasasa rewards checking accounts, and access to local loan officers. Primary caveat: the bank operates only in specific Arkansas locations and lacks the national footprint, online-exclusive rates, or technology-first approach of digital-native banks, making it less suitable for customers prioritizing maximum convenience or rate shopping across national lenders.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
